parsed.production_notes: The top panel features an AI-generated avatar with perfectly smooth skin, a static background, and slightly unnatural lip-sync. The bottom panel is human-recorded, showing natural movements, real props, and a visible lavalier microphone.

characters.appearance: Young adult female, slim build, fair complexion. Short blonde bob haircut. Symmetrical facial features.
characters.wardrobe: Beige blazer over a light-colored top, delicate silver necklace.

characters.appearance: Young adult female, slim build, fair complexion. Short reddish-brown hair with bangs.
characters.wardrobe: White spaghetti-strap top, black lavalier microphone clipped to the neckline.
characters.demeanor: Expressive, slightly confused initially, then surprised and engaged.

scenes.setting: Split screen: blurred office (top) and bedroom with plant (bottom).

panels.panel_visual_description: Character A faces the camera directly in a medium close-up. She maintains a steady, attentive gaze with minimal head movement. Her posture is upright and professional. The background is a softly blurred interior space with muted, cool-toned lighting.

Character B is framed in a medium close-up, looking slightly downward. She holds a large yellow glass candle in one hand and a lit lighter in the other. She brings the flame near the candle wick. The background shows a room with a wooden wardrobe and a large green leafy plant. The lighting is natural and warm, casting soft shadows.

scenes.visual_description: A split-screen layout with an AI avatar on top and a human learner on the bottom. The human interacts with props while conversing with the avatar. Text overlays and UI elements appear throughout the scene.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: What are you doing?
Character B says: I try to burn the candle.
Character A says: You mean I'm trying to light the candle?
Character B says: Okay. I'm trying to light the candle. I really like the smell of it.
Character A says: Don't say "smell". In English smell usually means bad smells. You should say "scent".
Character B says: Okay. I really love the scent of the candle.
Character A says: Good job!
Character B says: I really need an English speaking practice!
Character A says: You should speak to a native every day.
Character B says: I don't have money to pay for a tutor!
Character A says: Then speak to AI tutors.
Character B says: I don't want to talk to an AI.
Character A says: Surprise! I'm an AI teacher!
Character B says: You? You're really good!
Character A says: It's like talking to a human. Just download the Fluently app. We will talk everyday.
Character B says: Okay. So I'll just download Fluently and practice English everyday!
